video_title: Guy Fieri Feasts On From-Scratch Bagels in NOLA 🥯 | Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives | Food Network

restaurants.name: Suri's
restaurants.guy_intro: Here I am in New Orleans, so you're expecting Bourbon Street jambalaya and beignets. But no, no, no. Today you get a neighborhood and a juice bar that makes homemade bagels and lox. Because here at Suri's, it's all about owner Greg Suri's favorites.

dishes.description: Homemade bagels, boiled and baked like in New York, served with fresh, salty lox and cream cheese.
dishes.guy_response: It's wonderful. Plus the lox are nice and salty. It's a whole different animal when they're fresh like that. Oh, Lord. Mmm. Tastes fresh. It's much more doughy. You can tell that it's homemade. Lox are great. It's ridiculously good. The texture's perfect. Goes well with the cream cheese. Really, really good. I could sit there and eat that whole thing.

dishes.dish_name: Corned Beef Hash with Andouille and Boudin Sausage
dishes.description: Homemade corned beef, smoked boudin sausage, andouille sausage, rosemary, pre-roasted potatoes, and Cajun seasoning.
dishes.guy_response: That is great. Wow, that's good. That's hash-alicious. Top three best hashes. It's nice and chunky. That's what I like about it.

dishes.dish_name: Apple Ginger Juice with Wheatgrass Shooter
dishes.description: Fresh squeezed apple ginger juice with an optional wheatgrass shooter.
dishes.guy_response: Wow, you pick up the ginger in that. It's not a sipping kind of beverage. It's good. I mean, it's tasty. It's a little shocking at first. It's amazing.

restaurants.name: Humble Wood-Fired Bagels
restaurants.guy_intro: So I'm here in Gainesville, Florida. I have been in this parking lot, 4th Avenue Food Park. No, I was looking for some wood-fired pizza, but then I heard the wood-fired pizza team also does bagels, and not just any bagels. The claim to fame is they're Florida-style bagels. Oh, I love me some bagels. This is Humble Wood-Fired Bagels.

dishes.description: Perfectly cured and smoked salmon lox on a toasted salted rosemary bagel with lemon dill cream cheese, capers, cucumbers, onions, and sprouts.
dishes.guy_response: Bagel's dynamite. Great chew. Really like the rosemary. A legit bagel. The salmon, the color's beautiful. A little bit of smoke to it. If you're a smoked salmon, lox and bagels kind of person, this is your game. And the sprouts on top are perfect. The smoked lox blends well with the dill, cream cheese that they use.

dishes.description: Roasted and marinated carrot lox on a toasted salted rosemary bagel with cashew cream cheese, capers, cucumber, pickled onions, and sprouts.
dishes.guy_response: First time I've had vegan lox. The carrot, that is a flavor bomb. That cashew spread sings right through. They're both dynamite. I would love to know their secrets for how they make those carrots. Because they've got that sort of smoky finish that you expect from a lox.

dishes.description: Crimini mushrooms, shiitake bacon, three olive cream cheese, mushroom tapenade, arugula, and Swiss cheese on an everything bagel, melted.
dishes.guy_response: If anybody gets bored by a bagel, come down here to Humble. You get this big umami bomb. You get some of the acid, some of the brininess, the three olive smear, really salty and briny cream cheese. I love the arugula on it. And on top of it all is the everything bagel underneath. Wow, that's good. The team here just does magic with mushrooms. The everything bagel is like a little bit crispy. It's perfect like that. This is a destination bagel joint.

restaurants.name: Big Bon Bodega
restaurants.guy_intro: So we're here in Savannah, Georgia, with the winner and only Iron Chef Stephanie Izard. We're here. This is Big Bon Bodega. You're going to find some flavor combinations here that you won't find anywhere else in Savannah. It's bagels and pizza, and it's the perfect place. This combo is brought to you by K-Heritage, who's doubling up oven duty. Bagels in the morning. And then it's pizzas in the afternoon.

dishes.description: Toasted sesame bagel with kimchi cream cheese, bulgogi meat, American cheese, pickled onions, and kimchi.
dishes.guy_response: The bagel is dynamite. It's got great chew. It's amazing. The bulgogi, you can put that on a flip-flop. But it's a really well-constructed sandwich. There's the right amount, considering the amount of bagel that there is. I mean, it's dynamite. The beef was so tender. And it just had a little bit of a spice. The combination of the meat and the kimchi, really excellent.

restaurants.name: Bagel Street Grill
restaurants.guy_intro: So when you end up here on the East Coast, what should you expect? That's right, Righteous Bagels. We're here in Plainsboro, New Jersey, and this is Bagel Street Grill. That's because owners and brothers, Jimmy and Doug Rohr, have been busting out bagels since they were teenagers.

dishes.dish_name: Jalapeno Cheddar Bagel with Vegetable Cream Cheese
dishes.description: Homemade jalapeno cheddar bagel, boiled and baked, with a serious kick, served with vegetable cream cheese.
dishes.guy_response: Brother, I've had a lot of jalapeno bagels, and all they do is waft the jalapeno over the top. You get serious. There's heat in that. I mean, you smell the jalapeno. That crust, that chew, soft in the middle, nice texture on the outside, real deal, brother. Real deal. Perfect crunch on the top. The cheddar has, like, a nice mildness to it that counteracts with the jalapeno.

restaurants.name: Bagelsaurus
restaurants.guy_intro: So I'm here in the Cambridge-Somerville area right outside of Boston to check out a joint where this chef was going to be a schoolteacher. Well, she kind of changed gears and decided to open a restaurant offering up one of my favorite things. It's something that takes so much time, so much energy, and it's really a labor of love. It's bagels. This is Bagelsaurus.

dishes.dish_name: BLT with Spicy Pepper Cream Cheese on Onion Bagel
dishes.description: Bacon, sweet oven-roasted plum tomatoes, red leaf lettuce, and spicy pepper cream cheese on a homemade onion bagel.
dishes.guy_response: The only thing that I don't like about this bagel is that one quarter of it's already gone. As a bagel. Of the best we've ever had on the show. Light, tender, great chew. Bacon's great, but these oven roasted plum tomatoes, the acid of that cut with the fat and the spiciness of the jalapenos and poblanos in the cream cheese, you're a dangerous woman. I'm not kidding you.
